title: Modified Archimedes Method for the Measurement of Average Porosity of Lithium-Ion Battery Electrodes and Its Comparison with Standard Techniques
authors: Rabin Siva Dev, Gerrit Karl Mertin, Marc Oldenburger, Dominik Wycisk, Jonas Günther, Ralf Moos and Jan Philipp Schmidt
abstract: This work presents a simple method for obtaining highly reproducible and precise values of the average porosity of electrodes used in lithium-ion batteries. The method was developed by modifying the classical Archimedes method (hence referred to as the Modified Archimedes Method or MAM) and employs dimethyl carbonate (DMC) as the immersion liquid to closely mimic electrolyte infiltration. All aspects of the method, measurement setup design, experimental protocol, error propagation, and validation of results, were systematically assessed. The wetting properties of DMC and battery electrolytes w…

title: BattScape, a Drag-and-Drop Electrochemical Battery Simulation Framework Implemented in MATLAB Simscape
authors: Rafał Pakuła, Williams Agyei Appiah, Mukul Parmananda, Bharatkumar Suthar and Johannes Landesfeind
abstract: Despite many commercial and academic approaches to simulate and predict battery operation, the complexity of software tools often limits their broad application. This work presents a novel physical domain library, called BattScape, that has been developed in Simscape from MathWorks® aiming at bridging the gap between experimentalists and simulation experts by providing a framework for user-friendly battery simulations. Our validation with an isothermal P2D Newman model with concentration dependent parameters in COMSOL Multiphysics® shows very good agreement for charge and discharge simulations…

title: Chemometric Models and Electrochemical Techniques for Studying the Health of Li-Ion Batteries: A Review
authors: E. Sandrucci, S. Brutti and F. Marini
abstract: This study presents a thorough analysis of machine learning techniques utilized for monitoring the health and predicting the remaining useful life (RUL) of lithium-ion batteries, which is essential for optimizing the performance of electric vehicles (EVs). Given the challenges faced by lithium-ion batteries, such as <mark>capacity fading</mark> and environmental factors, key indicators like State of Health (SOH) and RUL become vital. The paper reviews several adaptive machine learning methodologies, including Support Vector Regression (SVR), Gaussian Process Regression (GPR), and hybrid neural networks tha…

title: Revealing the Limitations of Lithium-Ion Batteries in Thermal Regeneration Electrochemical Cycle
authors: Yuansheng Xu, Yining Lao, Qingyun Dou, Shan Xu and Yuying Zheng
abstract: Harvesting low-grade environmental heat for electricity generation represents a sustainable strategy, but existing technologies struggle with efficiency and stability limitations. Although lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are mature for energy storage, their capability for direct thermal-to-electrical energy conversion remains largely unexplored. In this study, we investigate commercial LIBs within a thermal regeneration electrochemical cycle (TREC). Contrary to expectations for thermal energy harvesting, our results consistently demonstrate no net energy output, because the thermally induced volt…
